concerns.text: SC-001 requires validation against 'human-rated complexity scores from a pilot study (n=20)'. The spec defines the system's scope as processing video frames and running analysis, but contains no Functional Requirement (FR) mandating the collection, storage, or ingestion of these human ratings. Without an FR to acquire this data, SC-001 is unmeasurable because the reference data does not exist within the system's defined capabilities.

SC-003 requires measuring reaction-time difference against a 'baseline reaction time... for the same participant'. The spec defines 'ParticipantSession' but lacks an FR specifying a 'baseline condition' (e.g., a low-complexity control trial) that must be administered to every participant before the experimental trials. Without an FR to generate/record this baseline, the 'difference' metric cannot be computed.

SC-004 requires measuring the 'family-wise error rate' (FWER) against the nominal alpha. The spec mandates applying Benjamini-Hochberg (FR-004) but lacks an FR to *calculate and report the observed FWER* from the simulation or study results. FWER is a property of the *outcome distribution* across many tests/simulations, not a direct output of a single LMM run. The spec fails to define the procedure (e.g., 'run 1000 null simulations') to measure this.

SC-005 requires measuring 'stability of the effect size' across a sensitivity sweep. While FR-005 mandates the sweep, there is no FR defining the *metric of stability* (e.g., 'standard deviation of effect sizes' or 'range of significant p-values'). 'Stability' is a qualitative concept; without a quantitative definition in the FRs, SC-005 cannot be objectively verified.

User Story 2 (US-2) describes presenting clips and capturing NASA-TLX/reaction time. However, it lacks a specific FR or acceptance scenario for the 'counterbalanced order' (Latin Square) mentioned in the text. US-2 Acceptance Scenario 4 mentions counterbalancing, but there is no corresponding FR (e.g., 'FR-002b: System MUST generate a counterbalanced presentation order') to verify the implementation of this critical control variable.
